title: Best B.Tech Biotechnology College in Noida
content: B.Tech. in Biotechnology prepares engineers for fulfilling and dynamic careers in biotechnology industry, research, and allied professions. In the fields of medicine and pharmaceuticals, biotechnology is best known for its increasingly important role. It is also used in areas such as genomics, food production, and biofuel production. Currently, this course is in high demand in the Indian education scenario, with 157 AICTE approved colleges and universities in India offering the course. In Uttar Pradesh, 34 colleges are currently offering the course with a strength of 2096 students. For the course of B.Tech Biotechnology, an extensive integrated programme core and elective courses with laboratory research, training in current industry practices, and the soft skills necessary to excel in business, will be implemented to prepare the graduate students to address and lead the advances in research and development, allied biomedical research, healthcare, industrial biotechnology and more. The course will prepare students for an array of careers in Hospital and Healthcare Sector, R&D Sector, Biomedical Startups, Pharmaceutical Companies, PSUs, Sales and technical support, Biotech Companies, Clinical Research, Software Engineering, Medical Devices Companies and Bio-entrepreneurship. Minimum 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry & Mathematics/Biology/ Passed Diploma examination with at least 50% marks in any branch of Engineering and Technology. Or Passed B.Sc. Degree from a recognized University with at least 50% marks and passed 10+2 examination with Mathematics / Biology / Biotechnology as a subject. Or Passed B.Voc/3-year D.Voc. Stream in the same or allied sector. For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.E./B.Tech In Biotechnology Ist year minimum 50% marks or equivalent 2) 50% PCM/PCB or above at XII level 01 Biotechnology Knowledge and Technical Competence: To develop strong foundations in biological sciences, chemical sciences, and engineering principles, enabling graduates to analyze, design, and optimize biotechnological processes, products, and systems. 02 Innovation, Research, and Industrial Application: To train students to apply modern biotechnological, bioinformatics, and analytical tools for solving real-world problems in healthcare, pharmaceuticals, agriculture, and environmental biotechnology, fostering research aptitude and innovation. 03 Professional Development, Ethics, and Lifelong Learning: To prepare graduates for successful careers, higher education, entrepreneurship, and research through ethical practice, sustainability awareness, teamwork, and continuous learning in the rapidly evolving biotechnology sector. 01 Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of biological sciences, chemistry, mathematics, and engineering principles to solve complex problems in biotechnology. (WK1, WK2, WK3, WK4) 02 Problem Analysis: Identify, analyze, and provide solutions to biotechnological challenges using research-based approaches, statistical analysis, and computational tools. (WK1, WK2, WK3, WK8) 03 Design/Development of Solutions: Design and develop bioprocesses, biomaterials, and bioengineering systems with considerations for health, safety, sustainability, and societal impact. (WK4, WK5, WK6) 04 Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Use experimental research, bioinformatics, data modeling, and statistical tools to investigate complex biological and engineering problems and derive valid conclusions. (WK2, WK6, WK8) 05 Engineering Tool Usage: Develop and apply modern biotechnological tools, AI-based simulations, and analytical techniques to address biomedical, agricultural, pharmaceutical, and industrial biotech challenges. (WK2, WK6, WK8) 06 The Engineer and The World: Assess and analyze the impact of biotechnology on society, economy, health, safety, environment, and sustainability, considering global and cultural diversity. (WK1, WK5, WK7) 07 Ethics: Demonstrate ethical responsibility in biotechnology research, clinical trials, regulatory affairs, biosafety, and IPR compliance, ensuring adherence to national and international ethical frameworks. (WK7, WK9) 08 Individual and Collaborative Team Work: Function effectively as an individual, team member, or leader in multidisciplinary and cross-functional teams, including research collaborations and industry partnerships. (WK9) 09 Communication: Communicate effectively within scientific, industrial, and regulatory communities, including writing research reports, patents, and scientific publications, and delivering presentations. (WK7, WK9) 10 Project Management and Finance: Apply biotechnology project management principles, entrepreneurship strategies, and financial acumen to develop biotech startups, manage R&D projects, and drive industrial innovation. (WK6, WK8) 11 Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for continuous learning, adaptability to emerging biotechnologies, and critical thinking to contribute to biomedical, agricultural, pharmaceutical, and environmental advancements. (WK8, WK9) 01 Graduates will establish successful careers in biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, healthcare, food technology, bioinformatics, and environmental industries. 02 Graduates will pursue higher education and engage in cutting-edge research in fields such as genetic engineering, bioinformatics, molecular diagnostics, and regenerative medicine 03 Graduates will develop an entrepreneurial mindset, enabling them to launch biotech startups, innovate in biomaterials, biosensors, and nanobiotechnology 04 Graduates will serve in research and development (R&D) in academia and Biotech industries 01 Biotechnological Process Design & Development: Apply fundamental concepts of bioprocess engineering, genetic engineering, and biomolecular sciences to develop and optimize biotechnological products, biofuels, and industrial bioprocesses. 02 Computational and Analytical Proficiency: Utilize bioinformatics, computational biology, and AI-driven tools for genomic analysis, drug discovery, and diagnostics, enabling data-driven decision-making in biotechnology research. 03 Sustainable and Translational Biotechnology: Integrate knowledge of bioremediation, agricultural biotechnology, and food safety to develop eco-friendly and sustainable biotechnological solutions addressing global challenges in healthcare, environment, and industry. Minimum 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry & Mathematics/Biology/ Passed Diploma examination with at least 50% marks in any branch of Engineering and Technology. Or Passed B.Sc. Degree from a recognized University with at least 50% marks and passed 10+2 examination with Mathematics / Biology / Biotechnology as a subject. Or Passed B.Voc/3-year D.Voc. Stream in the same or allied sector. For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.E./B.Tech In Biotechnology Ist year minimum 50% marks or equivalent 2) 50% PCM/PCB or above at XII level
